senses.definition: The quality of confidently taking things for granted or acting as if you know more than you do.
examples: His assumptiousness in the meeting annoyed the more experienced staff.
There is a certain assumptiousness in the way she speaks to new employees.

senses.definition: Based on assumptions rather than proven facts.
examples: The report is highly assumptive and should be treated with caution.
We need data, not assumptive remarks about our customers.

senses.definition: In a way that is based on an assumption rather than clear proof.
examples: The author writes assumptively about cultures he has never visited.
They spoke assumptively, as if the results were already known.

senses.definition: A tendency to assume things or act on unproven beliefs.
examples: Her assumptiveness makes it hard for others to challenge her ideas.
The negotiator’s assumptiveness nearly ruined the deal.

senses.definition: Able to be made certain or guaranteed, especially by insurance.
examples: The company focuses only on assurable risks.
Not all potential losses are assurable under this policy.

senses.definition: A promise or statement that something will definitely happen or be true.
examples: The manager gave us his assurance that no one would lose their job.
Despite repeated assurances, the service did not improve.

senses.definition: A feeling of confidence and lack of fear or doubt.
examples: She spoke with calm assurance during the presentation.
His assurance grew as he gained more experience.

senses.definition: A type of insurance, especially life insurance, that pays money when someone dies or after a fixed period.
examples: They took out a life assurance policy after their first child was born.
Mortgage assurance is required by some lenders.

senses.definition: Plural of assurance: promises or statements that something will definitely happen or be true.
examples: We received assurances that our data would be protected.
Despite government assurances, many people remained fearful.

senses.definition: A person whose life or property is covered by insurance.
examples: The assurant must disclose all relevant information to the insurer.
The policy protects the assurant in case of serious illness.

senses.definition: Nonstandard or obsolete form, sometimes used humorously, meaning to make sure or to assure.
examples: He joked that he would assurate our success.
The writer used 'assurate' in a playful way to mean 'make certain'.

senses.definition: Nonstandard spelling of assured, meaning certain or guaranteed.
examples: Our success is assurd, he wrote in an old letter.
In the poem, the hero walks with assurd steps.

senses.definition: To tell someone confidently that something is true or will definitely happen, in order to make them feel less worried.
examples: I assure you, everything will be fine.
She assured him that help was on the way.

senses.definition: To make something certain to happen or to be achieved.
examples: Careful planning will assure success.
The new contract assures a steady income for the team.

senses.definition: To protect a person or property by insurance.
examples: They assured the building for a large sum.
The company decided to assure all employees against accidents.

senses.definition: Certain to happen or be achieved.
examples: With such strong support, her victory seems assured.
Continued funding is not assured.

senses.definition: Confident and relaxed, showing that you are sure about your abilities or your situation.
examples: She answered with an assured tone.
His assured manner impressed the interviewers.

senses.definition: A person whose life or property is protected by an insurance policy.
examples: The insurer must act fairly toward the assured.
The assured is entitled to full compensation under the contract.

senses.definition: Used to say that something is certainly true or will certainly happen.
examples: There will assuredly be more changes next year.
If you keep working like this, you will assuredly succeed.

senses.definition: A feeling or quality of being very confident and certain.
examples: Her assuredness on stage grew with every performance.
There was an air of assuredness in his voice.

senses.definition: Plural of assuredness: several types or examples of confidence and certainty.
examples: Different kinds of assurednesses arise from experience and knowledge.
The book explores the various assurednesses that leaders project.

senses.definition: People whose lives or property are covered by insurance.
examples: The company must treat all assureds fairly and honestly.
Losses will be shared among the assureds under this scheme.

senses.definition: A person or company that gives a promise, guarantee, or insurance.
examples: The assurer must pay the agreed amount if a claim is valid.
As the assurer, the firm carries significant risk.

senses.definition: Companies or people that provide insurance or formal guarantees.
examples: The market is dominated by a few large assurers.
Assurers must keep enough reserves to cover claims.

senses.definition: Third person singular form of assure: tells someone confidently that something is true or certain.
examples: She assures me that the documents are ready.
The company assures its clients that their data is safe.

senses.definition: To rise up like a wave; to surge upward.
examples: Dark waters began to assurge around the small boat.
Clouds assurged over the mountains as the storm approached.

senses.definition: Plural of assurgency: several acts or states of rising up, especially like waves.
examples: The sea’s assurgencies battered the coast all night.
The poet describes emotional assurgencies that cannot be controlled.

senses.definition: A rising up, especially in a strong, wave-like way.
examples: The assurgency of the tide surprised the fishermen.
There was an assurgency of emotion in the final chapter.

senses.definition: Rising upward, especially like a wave or a flame.
examples: She drew the assurgent curve of the mountain on the horizon.
Assurgent flames lit up the night sky.

senses.definition: Present participle of assure: telling someone confidently that something is true or certain.
examples: She is assuring customers that delays will be minimal.
They spent the afternoon assuring residents that the area was safe.

senses.definition: Giving confidence or comfort.
examples: She gave him an assuring smile.
His assuring words calmed the audience.

senses.definition: In a way that makes someone feel more confident or less worried.
examples: She nodded assuringly and took his hand.
The doctor spoke assuringly to the nervous patient.

senses.definition: A person or company that gives a formal guarantee or insurance.
examples: The assuror is liable for the loss under the contract.
Assurors must follow strict financial regulations.

senses.definition: Companies or people who provide guarantees or insurance.
examples: The new rules affect all registered assurors.
Assurors must keep detailed records of every policy.

senses.definition: Archaic spelling of assuage, meaning to make something less strong or severe.
examples: Nothing could asswage his grief after the loss.
They tried to asswage the villagers’ fears.

senses.definition: Past form of asswage: made something less strong or severe.
examples: Her fears were asswaged by his calm explanation.
The medicine asswaged the child’s pain.

senses.definition: Third person singular form of asswage: makes something less strong or severe.
examples: Time asswages many sorrows.
The news asswages his long-held doubts.

senses.definition: Present participle of asswage: making something less strong or severe.
examples: They were busy asswaging the anger of the crowd.
Asswaging her guilt proved difficult.

senses.definition: A rare mineral found in the Assynt region of Scotland.
examples: Assyntite was first identified in a remote part of northwestern Scotland.
The geologist added a small sample of assyntite to the collection.

senses.definition: An ancient region and empire in Mesopotamia, in parts of modern Iraq, Turkey, Iran, and Syria.
examples: Assyria became one of the most powerful empires of the ancient Near East.
Many artifacts from Assyria are displayed in major museums.

senses.definition: Relating to ancient Assyria or its people, culture, or language.
examples: The museum holds several Assyrian reliefs.
Assyrian art is known for its detailed stone carvings.

senses.definition: A person from the ancient region of Assyria or a member of the modern Assyrian people.
examples: Many Assyrians today live outside their traditional homeland.
An Assyrian wrote the inscription on this tablet.

senses.definition: To make someone or something adopt Assyrian culture or characteristics.
examples: The conquerors tried to Assyrianize the local population.
The policy aimed to Assyrianize neighboring regions.

senses.definition: Plural of Assyrian: people from ancient Assyria or members of the modern Assyrian ethnic group.
examples: The Assyrians built a powerful empire in the Near East.
Modern Assyrians maintain their language and traditions.

senses.definition: Relating to the academic study of ancient Assyria and Babylonia.
examples: She presented her findings at an Assyriological conference.
The museum collaborates with Assyriological institutes.

senses.definition: A person who studies the history, language, or culture of ancient Assyria and Babylonia.
examples: An Assyriologist translated the cuneiform tablets.
She trained as an Assyriologist at a European university.

senses.definition: A specialist in the study of ancient Assyria, especially in French-language contexts.
examples: The conference gathered many leading assyriologues.
As an assyriologue, he focused on religious texts.

senses.definition: The academic study of the history, languages, and cultures of ancient Assyria and Babylonia.
examples: He decided to specialize in Assyriology at graduate school.
Assyriology has provided important insights into early writing systems.

senses.definition: Resembling or related to Assyria or Assyrians.
examples: The building shows assyroid features in its reliefs.
Scholars described the statue as assyroid in style.

senses.definition: Scots law, historical: to compensate or make satisfaction for an injury or wrong.
examples: He agreed to assyth the injured party before the case went to trial.
The offender sought to assyth the family of the victim.

senses.definition: In historical Scots law, compensation paid to the relatives of a person who has been killed, in satisfaction for the wrong.
examples: The court ordered an assythment to be paid to the widow.
Assythment served as a form of private settlement in such cases.

senses.definition: Plural of assythment: several payments of compensation for injuries or killings under historical Scots law.
examples: Records mention multiple assythments agreed between the families.
The book traces how assythments changed over time.

senses.definition: Abbreviation for Atlantic Standard Time, a time zone used in parts of the Americas.
examples: The meeting is scheduled for 3 p.m. AST.
Halifax operates on AST during the winter months.

senses.definition: A female given name in some European countries.
examples: Asta greeted us at the door with a big smile.
They named their daughter Asta after her grandmother.

senses.definition: In electronics, describing a circuit that continuously switches between two states without staying in either state.
examples: An astable multivibrator is often used as a simple oscillator.
The engineer designed an astable circuit to generate the clock signal.

senses.definition: Relating to crayfish of the family Astacidae.
examples: Astacian species are common in European rivers.
The paper compares astacian and non-astacian crustaceans.

senses.definition: A crayfish belonging to the family Astacidae.
examples: The lake is home to several astacians.
Astacians are sensitive to changes in water quality.

senses.definition: A family of freshwater crayfish found in Europe, western Asia, and North America.
examples: Astacidae includes several well-known crayfish species.
Conservation efforts focus on native Astacidae in this region.

senses.definition: Relating to the scientific study of crayfish.
examples: He wrote an astacological survey of the local river system.
Astacological research has grown in recent years.
